_meta.variant_delta_from_v5: Softer directive text — conditional WITHIN the directive (if source has content, preserve; otherwise expand creatively). Gating on source-non-empty still present from V5.

girl_sex_distribution.interpretation: V6 REGRESSED vs V5 on girl_sex. Softer directive = less adherence = less explicit preservation. Mean dropped 3.7→3.2, stdev went UP slightly (0.9→1.1), success rate dropped 60%→40%. The soft 'if source has explicit content' language gives the LLM license to interpret what qualifies — and it often decides the prompt doesn't really warrant explicit.

miku_cake.notes: Slight improvement. Seed 42 missed hatsune_miku tag ('hands on another's chest' weird hallucination). Otherwise small quality bump.

girl_random_artist_random_era.notes: Marginal improvement vs V5. Still below V1. The softer directive still fires on thin 'girl' source.

empty_random_artist_random_setting.notes: Same as V1/V5 — directive skipped for empty source.

_summary.verdict: V6 REJECTED. Softer directive surrendered girl_sex win (-0.5 vs V5) for small miku_cake/girl_ra_re gains. Net: same as V1 baseline, worse than V5.
_summary.lesson_learned: Softness is the wrong axis to adjust. V5's strictness IS what delivers on girl_sex — a softer version lets the LLM 'decide' the prompt doesn't warrant explicit, which surrenders the win. The girl_ra_re regression needs a different mechanism (not directive softness).
_summary.next_hypothesis_v7: Lower prose-pass temperature (0.8 → 0.3) on V5 pipeline. Hypothesis: LLM variance at prose step is sampling-noise driven; lower temperature reduces that noise. Expected: girl_sex becomes more consistent (maybe at the cost of dice-roll creativity). Single factor: prose temperature.
